raw.objective: “This paper describes a developed pharmacokinetic model for the estimation of valproic acid (VPA) clearance (CL) calculated from routine clinical data taken from Egyptian epileptic patients.”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, WT. SEX, DDVPA, Comedication (CBZ, DDCBZ), INDI (indication) Included: AGE, DDVPA, CBZ, INDI
raw.observations: Model shows good predictive capacity in PRED scattergram. Model made with retrospective clinical data, backward deletion. No attempt was made to estimate relative F between formulations. They fitted a model with a bolus input and fixed the value of V at 0.22 l kg. A nonlinear relationship is found between CL and Age, WT, and DDVPA. In addition, VPA CL increased with comedication (CBZ) and in uncontrolled epilepsy patients.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.structure: one-compartment model, first order elimination and absorption
raw.equations: CL = ((theta1+(theta2*CBZ)+(theta3*DOSE)+(theta4*(AGE/20))+ (theta5*INDI)))*(1+eta1) V = theta6 Kel = theta7 KA = theta8 CHILD: KA=4 (oral tablet) ADULT: KA=1 (EC tablet) Cobs=Cipred *(1+eps1 ) DOSE: VPA dose in mg/day AGE: Age in years CBZ: comedication carbamazepine (Yes:1, No:0) INDI: Indication uncontrolled epilepsy (Yes:1, No:0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.105 theta2=0.151 theta3=0.000248 theta4=0.0968 theta5=0.0803 theta6=11.6 theta7=0.19 theta8=1 omega1=0.0529 sigma1=5.24

extraction_target.title: Pharmacokinetic modelling of valproic acid from routine clinical data in Egyptian epileptic patients.

structural_model.description: one-compartment model, first order elimination and absorption

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “This paper describes a developed pharmacokinetic model for the estimation of valproic acid (VPA) clearance (CL) calculated from routine clinical data taken from Egyptian epileptic patients.”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, WT. SEX, DDVPA, Comedication (CBZ, DDCBZ), INDI (indication) Included: AGE, DDVPA, CBZ, INDI

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
